assets.name: LangGraph
assets.description: LangGraph is a library for building stateful, multi-actor applications with LLMs. It extends LangChain with graph-based orchestration, enabling cyclical workflows, persistent state, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and fine-grained control over agent execution paths.

assets.name: CrewAI
assets.description: CrewAI is a Python framework for orchestrating role-playing autonomous AI agents. It models agents as crew members with defined roles, goals, and backstories, enabling collaborative task execution via structured pipelines and hierarchical or sequential process flows.

assets.name: AutoGen / AG2
assets.description: AutoGen (now AG2) is Microsoft's open-source framework for building multi-agent AI applications. It supports conversable agents, group chat orchestration, code execution, and flexible human-in-the-loop patterns, with a strong focus on LLM-driven task automation.

assets.name: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0
assets.description: Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 is the production-grade agent SDK underpinning Azure AI Foundry's agent capabilities. It provides declarative agent definitions, tool integration, memory management, and enterprise-grade orchestration patterns aligned with Azure's AI ecosystem.

assets.name: Claude Agent SDK
assets.description: Anthropic's Claude Agent SDK provides primitives for building multi-agent systems powered by Claude models. It includes managed execution loops, tool use orchestration, memory handoff patterns, and first-class support for the Model Context Protocol (MCP).

assets.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
assets.description: OpenAI's Agents SDK (formerly Swarm) is a lightweight Python framework for building multi-agent workflows. It introduces handoffs, guardrails, and tracing as core primitives, making it easy to compose agents that delegate to one another in a predictable manner.

assets.name: Google ADK
assets.description: Google Agent Development Kit (ADK) is an open-source framework for building, evaluating, and deploying AI agents with Gemini models. It supports multi-agent architectures, built-in tools, streaming responses, and seamless integration with Google Cloud infrastructure.

assets.name: PydanticAI
assets.description: PydanticAI is a Python agent framework built by the Pydantic team that brings type safety and validation-first design to AI agent development. It integrates natively with Pydantic models for structured outputs, supports multiple LLM backends, and provides dependency injection for agent tools.

assets.name: DSPy
assets.description: DSPy is a framework for algorithmically optimizing LM prompts and weights. Rather than hand-crafting prompts, DSPy compiles declarative modules—including retrieval, chain-of-thought, and agent loops—into optimized prompt programs using automatic compilation and teleprompters.

assets.name: Haystack
assets.description: Haystack by deepset is an open-source framework for building production-ready LLM applications, RAG pipelines, and agents. It features a composable pipeline architecture, built-in document stores, retrieval components, and a growing ecosystem of integrations.

assets.name: Smolagents
assets.description: Smolagents is Hugging Face's minimal yet powerful agent library that lets LLMs write and execute Python code to solve tasks. It supports code agents, tool-calling agents, and multi-agent hierarchies with a focus on simplicity and transparency.

assets.name: LlamaIndex
assets.description: LlamaIndex is a data framework for building LLM-powered applications with a focus on data ingestion, indexing, and querying. It provides agent abstractions including ReAct, function-calling, and multi-agent workflows, as well as a rich ecosystem of data connectors.

assets.name: Magentic-One
assets.description: Magentic-One is Microsoft Research's generalist multi-agent system for solving complex tasks. It uses an Orchestrator agent directing specialized sub-agents (web browser, code executor, file handler) and achieves state-of-the-art performance on agentic benchmarks.

assets.name: Claude Code
assets.description: Claude Code is Anthropic's agentic coding assistant that operates directly in the terminal. It reads codebases, edits files, runs tests, manages git, and executes shell commands autonomously, with support for MCP tools, custom agents, and persistent memory via CLAUDE.md.

assets.name: Cursor
assets.description: Cursor is an AI-native code editor built on VS Code that integrates LLMs deeply into the development workflow. It offers inline completions, multi-file chat, agent mode for autonomous task execution, and codebase-wide context via semantic search.

assets.name: Windsurf
assets.description: Windsurf (by Codeium) is an agentic IDE that introduces Cascade, an AI agent with deep awareness of the developer's actions and codebase state. Cascade can autonomously write code, run commands, fix bugs, and maintain coherent multi-step workflows across files.

assets.name: Devin
assets.description: Devin is Cognition's fully autonomous software engineer agent. It operates in a sandboxed environment with browser, terminal, and editor access, capable of completing end-to-end engineering tasks from issue triage to deployment with minimal human supervision.

assets.name: OpenAI Codex
assets.description: OpenAI Codex is a cloud-based software engineering agent that runs in isolated sandboxes, executing tasks in parallel from natural language instructions. It integrates with GitHub to read repositories, write code, run tests, and create PRs autonomously.

assets.name: Gemini CLI
assets.description: Gemini CLI is Google's open-source terminal-based AI agent powered by Gemini models. It provides interactive and scriptable access to Gemini's capabilities directly from the command line, with tool use, file context, and integration with Google services.

assets.name: Aider
assets.description: Aider is an open-source command-line coding assistant that pairs with LLMs to edit code in local git repositories. It supports architect-editor multi-model workflows, automatic git commits, and works with dozens of LLMs including Claude, GPT-4, and local models.

assets.name: Cline
assets.description: Cline is an open-source autonomous coding agent that runs as a VS Code extension. It can create and edit files, execute terminal commands, use the browser, and call MCP tools—all with a transparent approval workflow showing every action before execution.

assets.name: Kiro
assets.description: Kiro is Amazon's spec-driven AI IDE that introduces a structured approach to agentic development. It generates product requirements, design documents, and task lists before writing code, combining planning rigor with autonomous implementation and steering capability.

assets.name: Replit Agent
assets.description: Replit Agent is a browser-based AI software engineer that builds, deploys, and iterates on full-stack applications in the cloud. It handles environment setup, dependency installation, debugging, and deployment within Replit's managed cloud infrastructure.

assets.name: GitHub Copilot
assets.description: GitHub Copilot is Microsoft's AI coding assistant integrated across GitHub, VS Code, and other IDEs. It provides inline completions, chat-based assistance, and an autonomous coding agent mode (Copilot Workspace) for planning and implementing changes across repositories.

assets.name: Amazon Q Developer
assets.description: Amazon Q Developer is AWS's AI coding assistant with deep AWS service knowledge. It offers inline completions, chat, security scanning, code transformation, and autonomous agent capabilities for tasks like upgrading Java versions and generating unit tests at scale.

assets.name: A2A Protocol
assets.description: Agent-to-Agent (A2A) Protocol is Google's open standard for communication between AI agents across different platforms and vendors. It defines a JSON-based wire format for task delegation, capability discovery via Agent Cards, and streaming result delivery.

assets.name: Model Context Protocol
assets.description: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is Anthropic's open standard for connecting AI models to external tools, data sources, and services. It defines a client-server architecture with typed resources, prompts, and tools, enabling any MCP-compatible host to access any MCP server.

assets.name: ACP
assets.description: Agent Communication Protocol (ACP) is IBM's open specification for standardized REST-based communication between AI agents. It defines how agents expose capabilities, receive tasks, and return results, enabling cross-platform interoperability without SDK dependencies.

assets.name: ANP
assets.description: Agent Network Protocol (ANP) is an open protocol for building decentralized networks of AI agents. It provides identity, discovery, and communication primitives that allow agents to find and collaborate with each other across organizational and platform boundaries.

assets.name: LangGraph Cloud
assets.description: LangGraph Cloud is LangChain's managed platform for deploying, scaling, and monitoring LangGraph agents in production. It provides persistent state storage, horizontal scaling, built-in checkpointing, human-in-the-loop support, and a studio UI for visualization.

assets.name: CrewAI Enterprise
assets.description: CrewAI Enterprise is the managed cloud platform for deploying CrewAI agent pipelines at scale. It adds enterprise features including crew management UI, RBAC, audit logs, deployment pipelines, and production-grade monitoring on top of the open-source CrewAI framework.

assets.name: AutoGen Studio
assets.description: AutoGen Studio is a low-code interface for building, testing, and debugging multi-agent workflows powered by AutoGen. It provides a drag-and-drop agent builder, team configuration, skill library, and visual execution traces for rapid prototyping of agent systems.

assets.name: Prefect
assets.description: Prefect is a modern workflow orchestration platform that brings reliability and observability to Python-based data and AI pipelines. It supports dynamic DAGs, retries, caching, scheduling, and real-time monitoring, making it suitable for orchestrating agent task graphs.

assets.name: Temporal
assets.description: Temporal is a durable execution platform for building resilient, long-running workflows. It persists workflow state across failures, supports retries, timeouts, and human-in-the-loop patterns, making it a strong backend for agentic task execution with guaranteed completion.

assets.name: OpenAI Swarm
assets.description: OpenAI Swarm is an experimental, lightweight multi-agent orchestration framework emphasizing handoffs and routines. It is designed for educational purposes to illustrate multi-agent patterns, and has since evolved into the production-grade OpenAI Agents SDK.

assets.name: Filesystem MCP Server
assets.description: The official Filesystem MCP server provides AI models with secure, configurable access to the local file system. It exposes tools for reading, writing, moving, and searching files within allowed directories, with path traversal protections built in.

assets.name: GitHub MCP Server
assets.description: The GitHub MCP server exposes GitHub's API as MCP tools, enabling AI agents to manage repositories, issues, pull requests, branches, files, and code search. It is the primary integration layer for agentic software development workflows on GitHub.

assets.name: PostgreSQL MCP Server
assets.description: The PostgreSQL MCP server provides AI models with read and write access to PostgreSQL databases. It exposes schema inspection, SQL query execution, and table management tools, enabling agents to query, analyze, and modify relational data safely.

assets.name: Playwright MCP Server
assets.description: The Playwright MCP server exposes browser automation capabilities to AI agents via MCP. It supports navigation, clicking, typing, screenshotting, and structured accessibility tree extraction, enabling agents to interact with any web application programmatically.

assets.name: Slack MCP Server
assets.description: The Slack MCP server gives AI agents the ability to interact with Slack workspaces. It supports reading channels, posting messages, managing threads, listing users, and responding to workspace events, enabling agentic communication workflows.

assets.name: Memory MCP Server
assets.description: The Memory MCP server provides a knowledge graph-based persistent memory layer for AI agents. It allows agents to store entities, relationships, and observations across sessions, enabling continuity and contextual recall without relying on conversation history.

assets.name: Google Drive MCP Server
assets.description: The Google Drive MCP server enables AI agents to search, read, and interact with files stored in Google Drive. It supports Google Docs, Sheets, and Slides content extraction, making cloud-stored documents accessible within agentic workflows.

assets.name: Brave Search MCP Server
assets.description: The Brave Search MCP server integrates Brave's independent web search index into AI agent workflows. It provides real-time web search and local search capabilities without tracking, making it a privacy-respecting tool-use endpoint for information retrieval.

assets.name: Sentry MCP Server
assets.description: The Sentry MCP server connects AI agents to Sentry's error monitoring platform. Agents can query issues, inspect stack traces, retrieve event details, and analyze error trends—enabling AI-assisted debugging workflows grounded in real production error data.

assets.name: Firecrawl MCP Server
assets.description: The Firecrawl MCP server exposes Firecrawl's web scraping and crawling capabilities to AI agents. It converts websites into clean LLM-ready Markdown, handles JavaScript rendering, supports crawl maps and deep scraping, and is optimized for RAG and agent ingestion workflows.

assets.name: Notion MCP Server
assets.description: The Notion MCP server provides AI agents with access to Notion workspaces. It supports reading and writing pages, databases, and blocks, enabling agents to manage knowledge bases, project trackers, and documentation stored in Notion.

assets.name: Linear MCP Server
assets.description: The Linear MCP server connects AI agents to Linear's project management platform. Agents can create, read, and update issues, manage cycles, query team backlogs, and move tasks through workflows, enabling AI-driven engineering project management.

assets.name: Supabase MCP Server
assets.description: The Supabase MCP server gives AI agents direct access to Supabase projects. It supports database queries, Edge Function management, storage operations, and project configuration, enabling agents to build and manage full-stack applications on Supabase.

assets.name: SQLite MCP Server
assets.description: The SQLite MCP server provides AI agents with full access to local SQLite databases. It supports schema inspection, query execution, table creation, and data analysis, offering a lightweight embedded database tool for agent-driven data workflows.

assets.name: Puppeteer MCP Server
assets.description: The Puppeteer MCP server enables AI agents to automate Chrome or Chromium browsers using Puppeteer. It supports page navigation, element interaction, screenshots, and JavaScript execution, making it suitable for web scraping and browser automation workflows.

assets.name: Docker MCP Server
assets.description: The Docker MCP server exposes Docker's container management capabilities to AI agents. Agents can list, start, stop, and inspect containers, manage images, read logs, and execute commands in running containers, enabling agent-driven DevOps workflows.

assets.name: AWS MCP Server
assets.description: The AWS MCP server provides AI agents with access to Amazon Web Services through the AWS SDK. It exposes tools for managing S3, Lambda, EC2, and other AWS services, enabling infrastructure management, cost analysis, and cloud operations via natural language.

assets.name: Cloudflare MCP Server
assets.description: The Cloudflare MCP server connects AI agents to Cloudflare's developer platform. It exposes Workers, KV, R2, D1, and other Cloudflare primitives as MCP tools, enabling agents to deploy code, manage storage, and configure CDN settings programmatically.

assets.name: Stripe MCP Server
assets.description: The Stripe MCP server exposes Stripe's payment and billing API to AI agents. Agents can query customers, subscriptions, invoices, and payment intents, enabling AI-assisted financial operations, customer support, and billing workflow automation.

assets.name: Resend MCP Server
assets.description: The Resend MCP server enables AI agents to send transactional emails via the Resend platform. Agents can compose and dispatch emails with React-based templates, manage domains, and track delivery, making email a first-class action in agentic workflows.

assets.name: Twilio MCP Server
assets.description: The Twilio MCP server connects AI agents to Twilio's communication platform. Agents can send SMS, make voice calls, manage phone numbers, and interact with Twilio's messaging APIs, enabling communication-driven agent workflows.

assets.name: Jira MCP Server
assets.description: The Jira MCP server provides AI agents with access to Atlassian Jira projects. Agents can create, read, update, and transition issues, query backlogs, manage sprints, and generate reports, enabling AI-driven project management and ticket triage workflows.

assets.name: Google Calendar MCP Server
assets.description: The Google Calendar MCP server allows AI agents to read and manage Google Calendar events. Agents can list calendars, create events, update scheduling, respond to invitations, and find available time slots, enabling AI-driven calendar management.

assets.name: Gmail MCP Server
assets.description: The Gmail MCP server enables AI agents to interact with Gmail accounts. It supports searching, reading, drafting, and sending emails as well as managing labels and threads, making it possible to build AI-powered email management and automation workflows.

assets.name: MongoDB MCP Server
assets.description: The MongoDB MCP server exposes MongoDB's document database to AI agents. It supports querying collections, inserting and updating documents, schema inspection, and aggregation pipelines, enabling agents to interact with NoSQL data stores naturally.

assets.name: Redis MCP Server
assets.description: The Redis MCP server connects AI agents to Redis key-value stores. Agents can get, set, delete, and scan keys; work with hashes, lists, and sets; and interact with Redis Streams, enabling use of Redis as a shared state, cache, and message broker in agent systems.

assets.name: Kubernetes MCP Server
assets.description: The Kubernetes MCP server gives AI agents control over Kubernetes clusters. Agents can list and describe resources, apply manifests, read logs, manage deployments, and execute commands in pods, enabling AI-driven Kubernetes operations and incident response.

assets.name: Vercel MCP Server
assets.description: The Vercel MCP server allows AI agents to manage Vercel deployments and projects. Agents can trigger deploys, inspect build logs, manage domains, configure environment variables, and monitor deployment status, integrating Vercel into agentic CI/CD workflows.

assets.name: Apify MCP Server
assets.description: The Apify MCP server connects AI agents to Apify's web scraping and automation platform. Agents can run Actors for structured data extraction from any website, store results in datasets, and trigger automation workflows from within their reasoning loops.

assets.name: Semgrep MCP Server
assets.description: The Semgrep MCP server exposes Semgrep's static analysis engine to AI agents. Agents can run security scans, query vulnerability findings, and interpret SAST results for specific files or codebases, integrating security review into agentic development workflows.

assets.name: MCP Python SDK
assets.description: The official MCP Python SDK provides everything needed to build MCP servers and clients in Python. It includes high-level decorators for exposing tools, resources, and prompts; low-level transport adapters (stdio, SSE, Streamable HTTP); and async-first design throughout.

assets.name: MCP TypeScript SDK
assets.description: The official MCP TypeScript SDK enables developers to build MCP servers and clients in TypeScript and JavaScript. It provides typed interfaces for tools, resources, and prompts, with built-in support for stdio, SSE, and Streamable HTTP transports.

assets.name: FastMCP
assets.description: FastMCP is a high-level Python framework for building MCP servers quickly. Inspired by FastAPI, it uses decorators and type annotations to define tools, resources, and prompts with minimal boilerplate, dramatically reducing the time to build a production-ready MCP server.

assets.name: Smithery
assets.description: Smithery is a registry and deployment platform for MCP servers. It allows developers to publish, discover, and install MCP servers from a central index, and provides hosted deployment so servers can be accessed without local setup.

assets.name: Glama
assets.description: Glama is an AI gateway and MCP server marketplace that provides unified access to LLMs and tools. It indexes hundreds of MCP servers, provides an MCP client playground, and offers routing and caching infrastructure for AI-powered applications.

assets.name: Composio
assets.description: Composio is a tool integration platform that provides 250+ pre-built integrations for AI agents, exposed via MCP, OpenAI function calling, and direct SDK access. It handles OAuth, credential management, and action execution across SaaS apps, APIs, and dev tools.

assets.name: PulseMCP
assets.description: PulseMCP is a directory and discovery platform for MCP servers, tracking the rapidly expanding ecosystem. It indexes servers by category with stats, ratings, and installation instructions, making it the go-to resource for finding production-ready MCP integrations.

assets.name: mcp.so
assets.description: mcp.so is a community-driven directory of MCP servers that aggregates hundreds of integrations with search, filtering, and one-click setup instructions. It serves as a discoverable index of the MCP ecosystem for both developers and end users.

assets.name: mcpservers.org
assets.description: mcpservers.org is a curated open-source directory of MCP servers with categorized listings, GitHub star counts, and compatibility tags. It focuses on helping developers quickly locate servers for specific use cases across databases, productivity tools, and cloud platforms.

assets.name: Cloudflare MCP Hosting
assets.description: Cloudflare's MCP hosting solution enables developers to deploy MCP servers as Cloudflare Workers at the edge. It provides low-latency, globally distributed MCP endpoints with OAuth integration, Durable Objects for state, and built-in Streamable HTTP transport support.

assets.name: Traefik MCP Gateway
assets.description: Traefik MCP Gateway is a proxy layer that exposes existing HTTP APIs as MCP-compatible tool endpoints. It enables teams to make any REST API accessible to AI agents without modifying the underlying service, translating MCP tool calls into standard HTTP requests.

assets.name: Streamable HTTP Transport
assets.description: Streamable HTTP Transport is MCP's modern network transport specification that replaces SSE for server-to-client communication. It enables stateless, resumable, and multiplexed MCP sessions over standard HTTP, making remote MCP servers practical at scale.

assets.name: Composio
assets.description: Composio is the leading tool integration platform for AI agents, providing 250+ authenticated integrations across CRM, productivity, DevOps, and communication tools. It abstracts OAuth flows, token refresh, and API versioning so agents can focus on task execution rather than integration plumbing.

assets.name: Toolhouse
assets.description: Toolhouse is a cloud-based tool execution platform for AI agents. It provides a managed store of tools that run serverlessly in the cloud, reducing latency and eliminating local infrastructure requirements for common agent actions like web search, code execution, and email.

assets.name: ToolRegistry
assets.description: ToolRegistry is an open-source Python library for managing and versioning AI agent tool definitions. It provides a centralized registry pattern for organizing tools across agent teams, with schema validation, versioning, and dynamic tool loading at runtime.

assets.name: Claude Tool Use
assets.description: Claude's native tool use capability allows models to call externally defined functions during inference. It supports parallel tool calls, multi-turn tool loops, and structured JSON schema definitions, forming the foundation of agentic behavior in Claude-powered systems.

assets.name: OpenAI Function Calling
assets.description: OpenAI's function calling feature enables GPT models to output structured JSON payloads matching caller-defined schemas. It supports parallel calls, strict schema enforcement, and is the basis for tool use in the OpenAI Agents SDK and all OpenAI-powered agent frameworks.

assets.name: Gemini Function Calling
assets.description: Gemini's function calling capability allows Gemini models to invoke external functions and APIs during generation. It supports AUTO, ANY, and NONE tool choice modes, parallel function calls, and bidirectional live-session tool use via the Gemini Live API.

assets.name: LangChain Tools
assets.description: LangChain Tools is a standardized abstraction for defining and using tools within LangChain agents and chains. It includes a library of 60+ built-in tools (web search, calculator, code execution), a tool decorator for custom tools, and toolkits for grouping related capabilities.

assets.name: Firecrawl
assets.description: Firecrawl is a web scraping API optimized for AI applications. It converts any URL into clean Markdown with a single API call, handles JavaScript-rendered pages, bypasses common anti-bot measures, and supports batch crawls and change monitoring for continuous data pipelines.

assets.name: Crawl4AI
assets.description: Crawl4AI is an open-source web crawler purpose-built for LLM applications. It produces clean, structured Markdown with semantic chunking, supports asynchronous parallel crawling, and includes content filtering and extraction strategies designed for RAG and agent ingestion.

assets.name: Apify
assets.description: Apify is a web scraping and automation platform offering 2000+ pre-built Actors for extracting structured data from any website. As a tool-use endpoint for AI agents, it enables reliable, scalable data collection from complex sites without maintaining scraping infrastructure.

assets.name: Mem0
assets.description: Mem0 is an intelligent memory layer for AI agents and assistants. It automatically extracts, stores, and retrieves relevant memories from conversations, supporting both user-level and session-level memory with a simple SDK that integrates with any LLM or agent framework.

assets.name: Zep / Graphiti
assets.description: Zep provides long-term memory for AI applications via Graphiti, its temporal knowledge graph engine. Graphiti continuously ingests conversational data into a graph structure, enabling complex relational queries over time-aware facts—critical for agents managing persistent user context.

assets.name: Letta / MemGPT
assets.description: Letta (formerly MemGPT) is an agent framework with a novel layered memory architecture. Agents manage their own memory hierarchically across in-context, recall, and archival storage, enabling agents to handle tasks that far exceed the context window of any single LLM call.

assets.name: LangMem
assets.description: LangMem is LangChain's memory SDK for building agents with long-term recall. It provides semantic memory extraction, cross-session storage, and retrieval primitives that integrate directly with LangGraph agents and LangSmith for observability.

assets.name: Chroma
assets.description: Chroma is the open-source AI-native embedding database. It provides a simple API for storing, querying, and filtering document embeddings, making it the most popular vector store choice for prototyping RAG pipelines and adding semantic memory to LLM applications.

assets.name: Pinecone
assets.description: Pinecone is the leading managed vector database for production AI applications. It delivers low-latency semantic search at billions of vectors, with real-time index updates, metadata filtering, namespaces, and enterprise-grade reliability for large-scale RAG and memory systems.

assets.name: Weaviate
assets.description: Weaviate is an open-source vector database with a GraphQL and REST API. It supports hybrid search combining dense and sparse retrieval, multi-tenancy, built-in vectorization modules, and a Weaviate Cloud managed service, making it suitable for production-grade memory systems.

assets.name: Qdrant
assets.description: Qdrant is a high-performance vector search engine and database written in Rust. It supports multiple vector types, payload filtering, sparse vectors for hybrid search, and on-disk storage, with a cloud-managed offering and excellent performance benchmarks for agent memory workloads.

assets.name: Supermemory
assets.description: Supermemory is an open-source memory API for AI applications, providing a unified interface to store and retrieve information from any source. It handles chunking, embedding, deduplication, and relevance ranking, offering a plug-and-play semantic memory backend for agents.

assets.name: Redis
assets.description: Redis serves as a fast, in-memory data structure store commonly used for agent working memory, session state, and short-term context caching. With Redis Vector Sets and RediSearch, it also functions as a vector store for semantic memory in production agent systems.

assets.name: LlamaIndex
assets.description: LlamaIndex is the canonical data framework for building RAG applications. It provides 160+ data connectors, a structured indexing system, multi-step query engines, and production-ready abstractions for combining retrieval with LLM reasoning in both Python and TypeScript.

assets.name: LangChain
assets.description: LangChain provides a comprehensive set of RAG primitives including document loaders, text splitters, embedding models, vector stores, and retrieval chains. Its LCEL expression language enables composing complex retrieval and generation pipelines declaratively.

assets.name: Haystack
assets.description: Haystack is deepset's production-ready framework for RAG and LLM pipelines. Its composable pipeline architecture supports hybrid retrieval, custom component development, and multi-modal inputs, with native integrations for major vector stores and LLM providers.

assets.name: RAGFlow
assets.description: RAGFlow is an open-source RAG engine focused on deep document understanding. It uses vision-based document parsing to extract precise chunks from PDFs, scanned documents, and mixed-content files, delivering higher retrieval accuracy than text-only chunking approaches.

assets.name: Vectara
assets.description: Vectara is a managed RAG-as-a-service platform that handles ingestion, indexing, retrieval, and generation in one API. It provides boomerang re-ranking, factual consistency scoring, and hallucination reduction features, making it suitable for enterprise knowledge assistant deployments.

assets.name: Unstructured
assets.description: Unstructured is the leading open-source library for parsing and chunking unstructured documents for RAG pipelines. It handles PDFs, Word documents, HTML, images, and 30+ file types, extracting clean text with structural metadata to maximize retrieval quality.

assets.name: Docugami
assets.description: Docugami applies AI to extract semantic structure from business documents, transforming contracts, reports, and forms into XML knowledge graphs optimized for RAG. Its domain-specific models understand document types rather than treating all PDFs as generic text blobs.

assets.name: Cohere Rerank
assets.description: Cohere Rerank is a cross-encoder model API for improving retrieval relevance in RAG pipelines. It takes an initial candidate set from a vector search and re-scores documents based on semantic relevance to the query, significantly improving answer accuracy over first-stage retrieval alone.

assets.name: DSPy RAG
assets.description: DSPy's RAG module provides an optimizable retrieval-augmented generation pipeline that automatically tunes retrieval and generation prompts via its compilation process. Unlike static RAG pipelines, DSPy RAG improves end-to-end task performance without manual prompt engineering.

assets.name: Cognee
assets.description: Cognee is an open-source memory and knowledge framework for AI applications that uses knowledge graphs as the retrieval layer instead of flat vector search. It builds rich entity relationship graphs from ingested data, enabling more precise contextual recall for complex multi-hop queries.

assets.name: Ragbuilder
assets.description: Ragbuilder is an open-source toolkit for automatically evaluating and optimizing RAG pipeline configurations. It runs grid search over chunking strategies, embedding models, and retriever parameters, reporting performance metrics so developers can choose the best configuration for their data.

assets.name: DSPy
assets.description: DSPy compiles natural-language programs into optimized prompt instructions using automatic few-shot example selection, instruction synthesis, and model finetuning. It eliminates brittle manual prompt engineering by treating prompts as learnable parameters in an optimization loop.

assets.name: Promptfoo
assets.description: Promptfoo is an open-source CLI and library for testing and evaluating LLM prompts. It runs prompts against multiple models and datasets, supports assertion-based evaluation, red-teaming, and CI integration, providing systematic quality assurance for prompt changes.

assets.name: LangSmith Prompt Hub
assets.description: LangSmith Prompt Hub is a collaborative prompt management platform integrated into LangSmith. Teams can version, share, and iterate on prompts with A/B testing, performance tracking, and direct integration into LangChain and LangGraph applications.

assets.name: Humanloop
assets.description: Humanloop is a prompt management and evaluation platform designed for enterprise LLM teams. It provides prompt versioning, collaborative editing, automated evaluation pipelines, human feedback collection, and model comparison to systematically improve prompt quality in production.

assets.name: PromptLayer
assets.description: PromptLayer is a prompt management and observability platform that acts as a middleware layer between application code and LLM APIs. It logs all prompt requests, supports visual prompt editing and versioning, and provides analytics on latency, cost, and quality across prompt versions.

assets.name: Portkey
assets.description: Portkey is an AI gateway and prompt management platform that provides centralized control over LLM usage across teams. It offers prompt versioning, A/B testing, routing policies, semantic caching, guardrails, and detailed observability for production AI deployments.

assets.name: Braintrust
assets.description: Braintrust is an end-to-end LLM evaluation and prompt management platform. It provides a prompt playground, automated scoring, dataset management, and CI/CD integration, enabling teams to run rigorous experiments and ship prompt improvements with confidence.

assets.name: Maxim AI
assets.description: Maxim AI is a prompt engineering and evaluation platform with a visual prompt studio, automated test generation, and multi-model comparison capabilities. It focuses on making prompt iteration fast and data-driven with built-in regression testing and output scoring.

assets.name: Mirascope
assets.description: Mirascope is a Pythonic library for building LLM applications with type-safe prompt engineering. It uses decorators and dataclasses to define prompts as code, supports provider-agnostic function calling, and integrates with Lilypad for prompt versioning and tracing.

assets.name: Agenta
assets.description: Agenta is an open-source LLMOps platform for prompt engineering and evaluation. It provides a collaborative prompt playground, automated evaluation pipelines, and experiment tracking, designed for teams that want full control over their prompt engineering infrastructure.

assets.name: LangSmith
assets.description: LangSmith is LangChain's observability and evaluation platform for LLM applications and agents. It captures full execution traces including every LLM call, tool invocation, and retrieval step, and provides an evaluation framework, dataset management, and production monitoring dashboards.

assets.name: Langfuse
assets.description: Langfuse is an open-source LLM engineering platform providing tracing, evals, prompt management, and metrics. It instruments LLM calls via a lightweight SDK, visualizes multi-step agent traces as nested spans, and supports self-hosted deployment for data privacy requirements.

assets.name: Arize Phoenix
assets.description: Arize Phoenix is an open-source observability platform for LLM applications built on OpenTelemetry. It provides trace visualization, embedding clustering, evaluation scoring, and retrieval analysis, making it the standard open observability stack for agent monitoring.

assets.name: Helicone
assets.description: Helicone is an open-source LLM observability platform that sits as a proxy between applications and LLM APIs. It captures every request with zero code changes, provides cost tracking, request caching, rate limiting, and user-level analytics for production LLM systems.

assets.name: Braintrust
assets.description: Braintrust provides end-to-end LLM evaluation and logging with real-time trace capture, scoring pipelines, and dashboard analytics. Its logging SDK instruments agent traces automatically, while its evaluation framework enables continuous quality monitoring against golden datasets.

assets.name: W&B Weave
assets.description: Weights & Biases Weave is a lightweight tracing and evaluation framework for LLM applications. It auto-patches popular LLM libraries to capture traces, provides an evaluation pipeline for scoring outputs, and integrates with the W&B ecosystem for experiment tracking.

assets.name: AgentOps
assets.description: AgentOps is an observability and testing platform built specifically for AI agents. It tracks every agent session, tool call, LLM interaction, and error with a session replay interface, cost analytics, and compliance recording for auditable agent behavior.

assets.name: OpenLIT
assets.description: OpenLIT is an open-source, OpenTelemetry-native observability platform for LLM and GenAI applications. It provides auto-instrumentation for 20+ LLM providers and frameworks, GPU monitoring, cost tracking, and a built-in guardrails system for production agent safety.

assets.name: Datadog LLM Observability
assets.description: Datadog LLM Observability extends Datadog's enterprise monitoring platform to AI workloads. It provides distributed tracing for agent workflows, cost attribution, quality evaluation scoring, security threat detection, and integration with Datadog's full APM and alerting ecosystem.

assets.name: Opik
assets.description: Opik is an open-source LLM evaluation and observability platform from Comet ML. It provides trace logging, evaluation metrics, prompt management, and A/B testing in a single platform, with both cloud and self-hosted deployment options for production monitoring.

assets.name: Lunary
assets.description: Lunary is an open-source LLM monitoring and analytics platform with a focus on production teams. It provides real-time trace capture, user analytics, feedback collection, prompt management, and alert policies, with a clean UI designed for non-technical stakeholders.
